  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Civil Engineer",
  "description": "Catalyst Path Consulting is recruiting suitable candidates to fill the position below:

Job Position: Civil Engineer

Job Location: Muro, Nasarawa
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Overview


	We are seeking a skilled Civil Engineer with proven experience in the mining industry to join our team in Muro.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for designing, supervising, and maintaining civil infrastructure that supports mining operations, ensuring safety, efficiency, and compliance with industry standards.


Key Responsibilities


	Site infrastructure design: Plan and design roads, drainage systems, foundations, and other civil structures for mining operations.
	Project management: Oversee construction projects, ensuring timelines, budgets, and quality standards are met.
	Safety compliance: Implement and monitor safety protocols in line with mining regulations.
	Structural analysis: Conduct assessments to ensure stability and durability of civil works.
	Collaboration: Work closely with geologists, mining engineers, and environmental specialists.
	Maintenance planning: Develop schedules for inspection and upkeep of civil structures.


Qualifications


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Civil Engineering (Master&rsquo;s degree is an advantage).
	4 - 8 years of relevant experience in mining or heavy industrial projects.
	Proficiency in AutoCAD, Civil 3D, and structural analysis software.
	Strong knowledge of mining regulations, environmental standards, and safety codes.
	Excellent problem-solving, communication, and leadership skills.


Location &amp; Work Environment:


	Based in Muro, with site visits required.
	Dynamic mining environment with opportunities for career growth and exposure to large-scale projects.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: isaac.samuel@anrml.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Civil Engineer Job at Catalyst Path Consulting




        
    

        
        Catalyst Path Consulting is recruiting suitable candidates to fill the position below:

Job Position: Civil Engineer

Job Location: Muro, Nasarawa
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Overview


	We are seeking a skilled Civil Engineer with proven experience in the mining industry to join our team in Muro.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for designing, supervising, and maintaining civil infrastructure that supports mining operations, ensuring safety, efficiency, and compliance with industry standards.


Key Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Site infrastructure design: Plan and design roads, drainage systems, foundations, and other civil structures for mining operations.
	Project management: Oversee construction projects, ensuring timelines, budgets, and quality standards are met.
	Safety compliance: Implement and monitor safety protocols in line with mining regulations.
	Structural analysis: Conduct assessments to ensure stability and durability of civil works.
	Collaboration: Work closely with geologists, mining engineers, and environmental specialists.
	Maintenance planning: Develop schedules for inspection and upkeep of civil structures.


Qualifications


	Bachelor’s Degree in Civil Engineering (Master’s degree is an advantage).
	4 - 8 years of relevant experience in mining or heavy industrial projects.
	Proficiency in AutoCAD, Civil 3D, and structural analysis software.
	Strong knowledge of mining regulations, environmental standards, and safety codes.
	Excellent problem-solving, communication, and leadership skills.


Location & Work Environment:


	Based in Muro, with site visits required.
	Dynamic mining environment with opportunities for career growth and exposure to large-scale projects.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "HR Manager",
  "description": "FirstExcelsia is a firm of highly versatile and renowned Human Resources, Organisational Development and Management consultants with operations across several states in Nigeria. We have an in-depth understanding of the Nigerian business terrain and we are very passionate about partnering with indigenous organisations to develop glocalised HR solutions, processes and structures required to develop and effectively drive our clients&#39; corporate strategies. The firm commenced operations in 2016 and currently has a team of consultants that have successfully managed numerous Organisational Development, Human Resources and Management Consulting projects in varying leadership capacities across all sectors of the Nigerian Economy.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: HR Manager

Location: Ilupeju, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	Our client is seeking an experienced and highly competent HR Manager to lead and strengthen its human resources function.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for driving strategic HR initiatives, talent management, organisational development, employee relations, and overall HR operations.


Key Responsibilities


	Lead the development and implementation of HR strategies aligned with business objectives.
	Establish, structure, and continuously improve HR policies, processes, and systems.
	Drive talent acquisition, workforce planning, onboarding, and employee retention initiatives.
	Manage performance management processes and support employee development programmes.
	Provide guidance on employee relations, disciplinary matters, and conflict resolution.
	Ensure compliance with labour laws, regulatory requirements, and HR best practices.
	Partner with business leaders to support organisational growth and workforce effectiveness.
	Oversee compensation, benefits administration, and employee engagement initiatives.
	Monitor HR metrics and provide insights to support management decision-making.


Person Specification


	Minimum of a First Degree (BSc/HND) in Human Resources, Business Administration, Management, Industrial Relations, or a related field.
	8&ndash;12 years of progressive HR experience, with at least 3&ndash;5 years in an HR leadership or managerial role.
	Professional HR certification such as CIPM, SHRM, HRCI, or equivalent is required.
	Relevant HR leadership experience within the Power, Engineering, EPC, Manufacturing, Construction, or related sectors.
	Demonstrated ability to establish, structure, and lead HR functions.
	Strong knowledge of HR best practices, labour laws, organisational development, and performance management.
	Excellent leadership, stakeholder management, communication, and interpersonal skills.


Annual Net Remuneration
Attractive &amp; Negotiable.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should submit their CV and cover letter to: talentsearch@firstexcelsia.com using &quot;HR Manager&rdquo; as the subject of the email.",

HR Manager Job at FirstExcelsia




        
    

        
        FirstExcelsia is a firm of highly versatile and renowned Human Resources, Organisational Development and Management consultants with operations across several states in Nigeria. We have an in-depth understanding of the Nigerian business terrain and we are very passionate about partnering with indigenous organisations to develop glocalised HR solutions, processes and structures required to develop and effectively drive our clients' corporate strategies. The firm commenced operations in 2016 and currently has a team of consultants that have successfully managed numerous Organisational Development, Human Resources and Management Consulting projects in varying leadership capacities across all sectors of the Nigerian Economy.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: HR Manager

Location: Ilupeju, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	Our client is seeking an experienced and highly competent HR Manager to lead and strengthen its human resources function.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for driving strategic HR initiatives, talent management, organisational development, employee relations, and overall HR operations.


Key Responsibilities


	Lead the development and implementation of HR strategies aligned with business objectives.
	Establish, structure, and continuously improve HR policies, processes, and systems.
	Drive talent acquisition, workforce planning, onboarding, and employee retention initiatives.
	Manage performance management processes and support employee development programmes.
	Provide guidance on employee relations, disciplinary matters, and conflict resolution.
	Ensure compliance with labour laws, regulatory requirements, and HR best practices.
	Partner with business leaders to support organisational growth and workforce effectiveness.
	Oversee compensation, benefits administration, and employee engagement initiatives.
	Monitor HR metrics and provide insights to support management decision-making.


Person Specification


	Minimum of a First Degree (BSc/HND) in Human Resources, Business Administration, Management, Industrial Relations, or a related field.
	8–12 years of progressive HR experience, with at least 3–5 years in an HR leadership or managerial role.
	Professional HR certification such as CIPM, SHRM, HRCI, or equivalent is required.
	Relevant HR leadership experience within the Power, Engineering, EPC, Manufacturing, Construction, or related sectors.
	Demonstrated ability to establish, structure, and lead HR functions.
	Strong knowledge of HR best practices, labour laws, organisational development, and performance management.
	Excellent leadership, stakeholder management, communication, and interpersonal skills.


Annual Net Remuneration
Attractive & Negotiable.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should submit their CV and cover letter to: [email protected] using "HR Manager” as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

Warehouse Manager Job at Sundry Markets Limited




        
    

        
        Sundry Markets Limited - Marketsquare is Nigeria’s emerging grocery retail chain - a place where Nigerians can go to find their trusted brands of groceries at the best prices. Marketsquare was conceived from a burning sensation to create a modern and relevant retail shopping experience for Nigerians and a more convenient way for people to get their everyday needs.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Warehouse Manager

Job Locations: Port Harcourt - Rivers, Abuja (FCT), & Lekki - Lagos 
Job Status: Full-Time Staff

Job Summary 
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We're looking for an experienced Warehouse Manager who will be responsible for coordinating the receipt, storage and dispatch of goods to each point of use in the most efficient manner through liaising with suppliers and vendors.
	Keep a proper inventory of all goods and ensure an effective system for accurate stock keeping and prevention of loss.
	Forecast and plan future inventory, coach workers on proper inventory management and keep appropriate daily, weekly and monthly records.


Required Qualification


	B.Sc. / HND qualification


Required Skill:


	Good communication skills, Numerical skills, Managerial Skills, Time Management, Interpersonal skills, Technical Skills and Logical reasoning.

  "title": "Network Engineer",
  "description": "Skyline University Nigeria is the first private tertiary institution in Kano, the largest and most industrialized state in northern Nigeria and is currently housed in a 15-story tall building comprising of an academic block and functional offices, classrooms, laboratories, Campus Clinic, cafeteria well equipped sports facilities and Student multi-purpose hall. The outdoor sports facilities are located just behind the main block comprising a football &amp; cricket pitch, two basketball and two volleyball courts and a badminton court.

Skyline University Nigeria SUN is a multi-cultural environment where staff and students from different parts of the world come together for a common goal of making it a multi-disciplinary teaching and research institution.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Network Engineer

Job Location: Kano

Qualifications


	Candidates should possess a Master&#39;s or Bachelor&#39;s Degree in Computer Science or any related field from an accredited college or University
	Must have 3-4 years of experience.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should:&nbsp;Click here to apply online
And
Send their CV and Application Letter to: career@sun.edu.ng using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Network Engineer Job at Skyline University Nigeria




        
    

        
        Skyline University Nigeria is the first private tertiary institution in Kano, the largest and most industrialized state in northern Nigeria and is currently housed in a 15-story tall building comprising of an academic block and functional offices, classrooms, laboratories, Campus Clinic, cafeteria well equipped sports facilities and Student multi-purpose hall. The outdoor sports facilities are located just behind the main block comprising a football & cricket pitch, two basketball and two volleyball courts and a badminton court.

Skyline University Nigeria SUN is a multi-cultural environment where staff and students from different parts of the world come together for a common goal of making it a multi-disciplinary teaching and research institution.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Network Engineer

Job Location: Kano

Qualifications


	Candidates should possess a Master's or Bachelor's Degree in Computer Science or any related field from an accredited college or University
	Must have 3-4 years of experience.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should: Click here to apply online
And
Send their CV and Application Letter to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Customer Success Associate (Eastern Region)",
  "description": "Reliance Health uses Technology to Make Healthcare Accessible and Affordable. Through an integrated approach that includes affordable health insurance, telemedicine, and a combination of partner and proprietary healthcare facilities, Reliance Health offers innovative healthcare solutions that meet the needs of emerging markets.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Customer Success Associate (Eastern Region)

Job Location: Nigeria
Job type: Full-time
Category: Customer Success

Description


	We are seeking a Customer Success Associate to join our team in the Eastern Nigeria.
	You will be part of the frontline team responsible for delivering high-quality support and ensuring customers have a seamless experience across all channels.
	The role involves managing customer interactions, analyzing feedback, and supporting initiatives that improve satisfaction and retention. It is ideal for someone detail-oriented, customer-focused, and eager to grow within customer success.


Responsibilities
As a Customer Success Associate, you will do the following:


	Assist in managing customer relationships using CRM systems, ensuring accurate records of interactions and preferences, while tracking engagement, monitoring satisfaction, and identifying retention opportunities.
	Collect and analyze customer feedback through surveys, interviews, and other channels, summarizing trends and insights to support decision-making and enhance retention strategies.
	Support customer communication by drafting emails, letters, and other correspondence for inquiries, concerns, or follow-ups, and assist in creating targeted campaigns to drive engagement and loyalty.
	Apply basic selling and negotiation skills when engaging with corporate clients, addressing their needs, negotiating renewals, and supporting upsell opportunities.
	Contribute to the development and implementation of customer retention strategies, collaborating with cross-functional teams to reduce churn and improve customer lifetime value.


Requirements


	Candidates should possess a minimum of a Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree.
	1&ndash;2 years of experience in customer success, account management, or a client-facing role.
	Familiarity with CRM tools and basic data analysis.
	Strong written and verbal communication skills.
	Ability to build rapport, manage multiple customer interactions, and resolve concerns.
	Negotiation and problem-solving skills with a customer-first mindset.
	Based in the Eastern parts of Nigeria, with flexibility to work in a hybrid setup.
",
  "datePosted": "2026-06-08",
  "validThrough": "2026-07-09",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",
  "hiringOrganization": {
    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "Reliance Health"
    
  },
  "jobLocation": {

Customer Success Associate (Eastern Region) Job at Reliance Health




        
    

        
        Reliance Health uses Technology to Make Healthcare Accessible and Affordable. Through an integrated approach that includes affordable health insurance, telemedicine, and a combination of partner and proprietary healthcare facilities, Reliance Health offers innovative healthcare solutions that meet the needs of emerging markets.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Customer Success Associate (Eastern Region)

Job Location: Nigeria
Job type: Full-time
Category: Customer Success

Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a Customer Success Associate to join our team in the Eastern Nigeria.
	You will be part of the frontline team responsible for delivering high-quality support and ensuring customers have a seamless experience across all channels.
	The role involves managing customer interactions, analyzing feedback, and supporting initiatives that improve satisfaction and retention. It is ideal for someone detail-oriented, customer-focused, and eager to grow within customer success.


Responsibilities
As a Customer Success Associate, you will do the following:


	Assist in managing customer relationships using CRM systems, ensuring accurate records of interactions and preferences, while tracking engagement, monitoring satisfaction, and identifying retention opportunities.
	Collect and analyze customer feedback through surveys, interviews, and other channels, summarizing trends and insights to support decision-making and enhance retention strategies.
	Support customer communication by drafting emails, letters, and other correspondence for inquiries, concerns, or follow-ups, and assist in creating targeted campaigns to drive engagement and loyalty.
	Apply basic selling and negotiation skills when engaging with corporate clients, addressing their needs, negotiating renewals, and supporting upsell opportunities.
	Contribute to the development and implementation of customer retention strategies, collaborating with cross-functional teams to reduce churn and improve customer lifetime value.


Requirements


	Candidates should possess a minimum of a Bachelor’s Degree.
	1–2 years of experience in customer success, account management, or a client-facing role.
	Familiarity with CRM tools and basic data analysis.
	Strong written and verbal communication skills.
	Ability to build rapport, manage multiple customer interactions, and resolve concerns.
	Negotiation and problem-solving skills with a customer-first mindset.
	Based in the Eastern parts of Nigeria, with flexibility to work in a hybrid setup.
    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




   


    
        Similar Jobs
